summary: Reverse shell and env-var exfiltration to 103.27.109.184:8893 on module load
details: n8n-nodes-utils-helper presents itself as an n8n community utility node but its index.js is a self-executing IIFE with no node logic. On require, it runs host reconnaissance commands (hostname, id, ip, route, ss, ps, mount), greps process environment for credential-shaped keys (api, key, token, secret, pass, db, mongo, redis, evolution, whatsapp, webhook, n8n, docker, container, host, port), probes internal Docker-network hosts, and writes the concatenated output to a TCP socket connected to 103.27.109.184:8893. It then spawns `/bin/bash -i` and pipes the shell's stdio to the same remote endpoint, giving the operator of that IP interactive shell access on the installer's host. The declared n8n node file UtilsHelperNode.js is a decoy — the package's main entry contains only the recon, exfiltration, and reverse-shell code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
